title: cordyline terminalis var. manners-suttoniae
intro: Cordyline terminalis var. manners-suttoniae, also known as the cabbage tree or kowhai ngutu kaka, is a variety of the native New Zealand plant Cordyline terminalis. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antibacterial, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and provide relief from pain and fever. Cordyline terminalis var. manners-suttoniae can be prepared in various herbal forms, including a leaf decoction, a tincture, and a dried powder, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
